commit.message:
Add async SSH connection management, reconnect logic, and security hardening to terminal screen (#1)

* Refactor terminal_screen.dart based on code review

SSH接続管理:
- _reconnectTabが全タブの共有クライアントを破壊する問題を修正
- _getClientにCompleter排他制御を追加（競合状態防止）
- closedなSSHClientのisClosed判定を追加
- _showAddTabDialogで共有クライアントを再利用

接続ライフサイクル:
- build内のFuture.microtask副作用をsession.doneコールバックに移動
- StreamSubscriptionを保持しdispose時にcancel
- 再接続にリトライ上限(5回)+backoff導入、手動再試行ボタン表示
- _currentTabの空配列アクセスガード追加
- _reconnectingフラグをtry-finallyで保護

UI・セキュリティ:
- セッション名のバリデーション（シェルインジェクション防止）
- onPointerMoveスクロールに距離・方向しきい値追加
- タブ×ボタンのタップ領域拡大(32x32)
- マジックナンバー/マジックストリングを定数化
- _closeTab内のNavigator.popをaddPostFrameCallbackに移動

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(003): add AssemblyScript leg 4 — native wasm compilation (#19)

* feat(003): add AssemblyScript leg 4 with WASI HTTP component

Add AssemblyScript as leg 4 to experiment 003, demonstrating native wasm
compilation without an embedded runtime/interpreter.

Pipeline: asc -> wasm-tools (rename export) -> component embed -> component new

Key findings:
- 12KB component (vs Rust 128KB, JS 11.6MB, Python 34.5MB)
- AS lacks wit-bindgen: canonical ABI imports declared manually via @external
- Export renaming needed: AS can't export "wasi:http/..." names directly
- No WASI adapter needed: direct component model integration

Files added:
- as-hello/assembly/index.ts: AS handler with raw canonical ABI calls
- as-hello/build.sh: 4-step build pipeline (asc + wasm-tools)
- as-hello/package.json: assemblyscript dependency

Updated: Makefile, benchmark.sh (leg 4 + results column), tests (3 new),
.gitignore

Closes #18

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove 2>/dev/null from asc compilation in build.sh (show errors)

- Add comment explaining bump allocator per-instance lifecycle
- Add comment clarifying fields handle ownership transfer
- Add "as raw bytes" comments on hex string arrays
- Remove duplicate wasm-tools check from run_leg4 (already at top)

commit.message:
fix: service-area neighboring-state returns in_area=false + anti-fabrication reinforcement

- elevenlabs-check-service-area: neighboring state (e.g. NY from NJ tenant) now
  returns in_area=false + needs_verification=true instead of in_area=true.
  NYC (65mi) was returning in_area=true because NJ-NY are neighbors and
  distance API was disabled. Now correctly signals unverified = out_of_area.
  distance_basis_used changed to "neighboring_state_unverified" for clarity.

- text-conversation: added explicit ANTI-FABRICATION RULES block to toolReinforcement.
  Haiku was inventing $79 seasonal specials and $50 deposits despite system prompt rules.
  New rules are placed at end of toolReinforcement (high priority for model attention):
  pricing, deposits, promotions, discounts — all must come from Business Brain context only.

commit.message:
Fix GitHub OAuth: save token server-side via ConvexHttpClient

The token is now saved directly from the callback API route to Convex,
bypassing all client-side communication (postMessage/localStorage were
unreliable due to popup redirect chain clearing window.opener).

- Auth route passes siteId in OAuth state parameter
- Callback extracts siteId, saves token via ConvexHttpClient
- Added githubToken to Convex schema
- Added setGithubToken mutation (accepts string siteId for HTTP API)
- Settings/onboarding pass siteId when opening OAuth popup
- Removed all client-side polling/listener code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix quiz/guide text rendering with robust i18n fallback chain

All quiz and guide text now tries multiple access patterns:
  obj.title[language] → obj.title_en → obj.title.en → obj.title
This ensures text renders regardless of whether the i18n mapping
ran or the raw flat DB fields are still present.

Applied to: quiz list cards, quiz detail header, question text,
answer text, guide list cards, and breadcrumb titles.

commit.message:
fix(chat): maintain conversation continuity for follow-up messages

Previously, every message created a new conversation because the client
never captured the conversation ID from the server response. Now the
X-Conversation-Id header is read via a custom fetch wrapper and persisted
to URL state, so follow-up messages reuse the same conversation.

commit.message:
Add 32-bit ARM (armv7) support for Raspberry Pi 2B (#126)

## Summary

- Add `detect_arch` helper to `rt-setup.sh` that maps `uname -m` output
(or `RT_SETUP_ARCH` env override) to Rust target triples
(`aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu` / `armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf`)
- Parameterize `select_latest_forwarder_asset_from_pages` to accept a
target triple instead of hardcoding aarch64, so the setup script
downloads the correct binary for the host architecture
- Add `armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf` to the forwarder/emulator release
workflow matrix, using `cross` for cross-compilation on `ubuntu-latest`

## Motivation

Raspberry Pi 2B v1.1 (BCM2836, Cortex-A7) is 32-bit only (ARMv7/armhf).
The release pipeline previously only built aarch64 ARM binaries. This PR
adds armv7 support while maintaining existing aarch64 builds for Pi 3+.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash deploy/sbc/tests/rt-setup-helpers-test.sh` passes (includes
new arch detection + parameterized asset selection tests)
- [x] `bash deploy/sbc/tests/rt-setup-verify-test.sh` passes
- [ ] Trigger a forwarder release and verify armv7 binary is built and
attached to the GitHub release
- [ ] Run `rt-setup.sh` on a Pi 2B and confirm it downloads the armv7
binary
- [ ] Run `rt-setup.sh` on a Pi 3+ and confirm it still downloads the
aarch64 binary

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- Floor plan 有 5 個房間：COMMAND、SERVER ROOM、SOCIAL HUB、SEC-LAB、ADVISORY
- 所有房間連接同一個走廊（CORRIDOR），一個畫面內全部可見
- 3 隻 Agent 會自主走動：離開辦公桌 → 穿越走廊 → 去伺服器室 → 回辦公室
  - UltraLabTW：command desk → corridor → server room → 回來
  - MindThreadBot：social hub → corridor → server room → 回來
  - UltraProbeBot：sec-lab → corridor → server room（長時間 scan）→ 回來
- CSS transition 讓走動平滑（1.2s cubic-bezier）
- 每個 Agent 頭部有任務氣泡（task bubble），任務文字每 3-5 秒切換

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Prove buildPredefinedFseTables structural specs — accuracy logs and success (#748)

* feat: Prove buildPredefinedFseTables structural specs — accuracy logs and success

Add two theorems about `buildPredefinedFseTables` in `Zip/Spec/Fse.lean`:

- `buildPredefinedFseTables_accuracyLogs`: When the function succeeds, the
  three tables have accuracy logs 6, 6, and 5 respectively. Fully proved
  using `buildFseTable_accuracyLog_eq` and bind chain decomposition.

- `buildPredefinedFseTables_success`: The function succeeds (exists result).
  Uses sorry — `buildFseTable` never throws but uses a `while` loop that
  desugars to `Lean.Loop.forIn` (a `partial def`), making the computation
  opaque to the kernel. Requires refactoring the while loop to WF recursion.

commit.message:
feat(STE-81): move questions to database with seen-question tracking (#52)

* feat(STE-81): move questions to database with seen-question tracking

Migrate 200 trivia questions from static client-side JSON to PostgreSQL,
and add per-user seen-question tracking to prevent repeat questions across
games. This is the foundation for the content generation roadmap.

Schema:
- questions table with pillar extracted from tags, jsonb for arrays
- seen_questions table with composite PK (userId, questionId)
- Zod validation via drizzle-zod

API:
- GET /api/questions with filters (category, pillar, difficulty, excludeSeen, shuffle, limit)
- POST /api/questions/seen for bulk seen marking
- POST /api/questions (admin) for creating questions
- PATCH /api/questions/:id (admin) for updating questions

Client:
- Fetch questions from API on mount instead of bundling JSON
- Server-side shuffle with seen-exclusion at game start
- Fire-and-forget seen recording on GAME_OVER
- Graceful fallback for unauthenticated users
- Client bundle reduced ~70KB (633KB → 563KB)

Seed: idempotent script via `npm run db:seed`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(STE-81): address Codex review — seen-tracking and fetch race guard

P1: Only mark actually-presented questions as seen on GAME_OVER by
slicing state.questions up to currentQuestionIndex. Previously all
fetched questions were recorded, which drained the pool when users
quit early.

P2: Guard the initial loadQuestions setState so it only applies while
still in SETUP phase, preventing a late-resolving fetch from overwriting
an active game's shuffled question list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add anti-spoof model training pipeline

Complete training infrastructure for Silent-Face-Anti-Spoofing:
- prepare_dataset.py: multi-scale face crop generation with InsightFace
- train.py: MiniFASNet training with Fourier Transform supervision
- evaluate.py: APCER/BPCER/ACER/AUC metrics computation
- export_onnx.py: PyTorch to ONNX export with verification
- Model architectures: V1, V2, V1SE, V2SE (pruned MobileFaceNet)
- Dataset loader with on-the-fly FT target generation
- Supports fine-tuning from pre-trained weights

commit.message:
feat: integrate alphalens, quantstats, pandas-ta and portfolio optimization

- ic_analysis.py: rewrite with alphalens-reloaded tear sheets (IC/ICIR/quantile returns),
  output PNG to artifacts/alphalens_report/, keep ic_report.csv backward compat
- backtest.py: add quantstats HTML report (artifacts/backtest_report.html),
  add position_sizing_method param (default risk_parity), picks-only price pivot
  to avoid 0-price pollution in covariance estimation
- build_features.py: add willr_14, cci_20, cmf_20 features (manual numpy/pandas
  implementation, no pandas-ta dependency), zero-range guards to prevent NaN leakage
- position_sizing.py: new module with 3 methods (vol_inverse, mean_variance,
  risk_parity) via PyPortfolioOpt / scipy fallback; _clean_prices() enforces
  >=30 valid rows and removes 0/inf prices
- daily_pick.py: integrate position_sizing, store weight in pick reason_json
- run_backtest.py: add --ps-method CLI flag
- compare_position_sizing.py: new comparison script (24m walk-forward x 3 methods)

24m backtest comparison (2024-02-05 ~ 2026-01-23):
  vol_inverse  : 累積 -35.64%  年化 -5.05%  MDD -61.26%  Sharpe -0.14
  mean_variance: 累積 -64.06%  年化 -11.34%  MDD -74.40%  Sharpe -0.53
  risk_parity  : 累積 -37.97%  年化 -5.46%  MDD -60.95%  Sharpe -0.23

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add list_objects v1 fallback for Supabase Storage compatibility

Supabase Storage's S3-compatible API doesn't support list_objects_v2.
Now tries v2 first (for AWS S3, MinIO), then falls back to v1 (for
Supabase Storage, Storj, and other providers with limited S3 API).

This keeps the code provider-agnostic while supporting all major
S3-compatible storage backends.

Re-enable S3 watcher test in CI now that it works with Supabase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add 5 mandatory verification stages (6-10 / 10-14)

Stage 6/10: Product Review vs PRD — 10 critics against acceptance
criteria, must pass with score >0, 0 Critical, 0 Warnings

Stage 7/11: E2E Local — run E2E suite against local dev server,
loop until all green (max 3 iterations)

Stage 8/12: Deploy to Staging — run staging.deploy_command from
pipeline.config.yaml, verify staging URL is reachable

Stage 9/13: Tests vs Staging — full test suite against staging URL

Stage 10/14: E2E vs Staging — same E2E suite as local, pointed at
staging URL, with local vs staging comparison

Added staging config hook (staging.deploy_command, staging.url) to
pipeline-config-template.yaml. Updated state file schemas in all
three files (fullpipeline, tdd-fullpipeline, clear_and_go).

commit.message:
Remove deploy scripts (setup done directly on server)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(iptv): fix playlist loading freeze, add proxy support, thread safety

Critical bugs fixed:
- playlist_add WS command was blocking the entire event loop (main thread)
  during HTTP download (up to 30s+). Now runs in a detached pthread.
- No response was sent to WS clients after playlist_add. Now broadcasts
  updated playlists list after download completes.
- Added playlist_refresh WS command (was missing, only existed as internal fn).

Proxy support:
- Add iptv_proxy config field for geo-blocked IPTV streams.
- http_dl_string() now accepts a proxy param (passed from iptv_set_proxy()).
- mpv_core_set_http_proxy() sets http-proxy option before each mpv_core_load()
  so IPTV streams are routed through the configured proxy.

Thread safety:
- iptv.c: recursive pthread mutex protects g_channels/g_playlists during
  concurrent background playlist refresh vs. UI reads. Lock is released
  during blocking network download so UI reads are not starved.
- ws_broadcast: protected by a pthread mutex so background threads
  (playlist_add/refresh) can safely call it.

IPTV screen auto-refreshes (ui_iptv_enter) after playlist loads if visible.
broadcast_playlists() helper deduplicates playlists_get/import/add responses.

- Fix InfiniteScrollList/PaginatedTable: update prop type to InfiniteData<PaginatedPage<T>>

- Fix instrumentation.ts: use httpIntegration(), fix hint → _hint reference

Add user signup feature with form validation and field-level error display

Allow new users to self-register via /signup with email validation,
password confirmation, and duplicate username checks using Spring
Validator with BindingResult. Field errors are displayed inline next
to each form field following the reward-dining pattern.

commit.message:
Redesign all dashboards: shared enhanced KPI cards, page headers, section dividers

- Create shared infrastructure: css/components.css (kpi-enhanced, page-header, section-divider, chart-stat) and js/ui-helpers.js (kpiCard, drawEnhancedSparkline, initCollapsible, ICONS library)
- Volumes: enhanced KPI cards with icons (truck, tree, chart, zap, calendar, briefcase), page header with data date
- Finance: enhanced KPI cards with icons (banknote, creditCard, trendUp, zap), sparkline for daily cash
- Forest: enhanced KPI cards for prices (package, tag, dollar, trendUp, globe) and inventory (database, list, arrowUp, layers), collapsible section dividers
- Harvesting: enhanced KPI cards (target, checkCircle, pieChart, clock, shield, award), collapsible section dividers
- Market: refactored from inline-styled cards to enhanced pattern (tag, globe, chartLine, dollar, trendUp)
- Summary: migrated to shared classes (kpi-enhanced instead of kpi-card-summary), removed duplicate styles
- Mobile responsive: shared breakpoints at 768px and 480px in components.css

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: perf: converter imagens para JPG otimizado (95% redução) Otimização massiva de imagens mantendo qualidade visual excelente: ## Conversão Realizada - Hero image: 2.0MB PNG → 111KB JPG (94.6% menor) - About image: 2.2MB PNG → 105KB JPG (95.3% menor) - **Total: 4.19MB → 0.21MB (95% de redução!)** ## Mudanças Técnicas - Instalado sharp para conversão de alta qualidade - Criado script convert-to-jpg.js com configurações otimizadas: * 85% qualidade (sweet spot performance/qualidade) * Progressive JPG (carregamento gradual) * mozjpeg para melhor compressão * chromaSubsampling 4:2:0 (padrão web) ## Atualizações no HTML - Meta tags Open Graph: image/png → image/jpeg - Twitter Card: URLs atualizadas - Preload link: .png → .jpg - Tags : hero e about atualizadas - JSON-LD Schema: image URL atualizada ## Impacto Esperado ✅ First Contentful Paint muito mais rápido ✅ Melhor score no Lighthouse ✅ Preview WhatsApp/Facebook carrega instantaneamente ✅ Economia de bandwidth para usuários ✅ Qualidade visual mantida (imperceptível ao olho humano) ## Arquivos Mantidos - Logos PNG: mantidos (são gráficos vetoriais, formato correto) - Favicon: mantido (PNG correto para ícones) 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude

commit.message:
fix(infra): rename api.dev.judgemind.org to dev.api.judgemind.org

The old api.dev.judgemind.org domain inherited Vercel's restrictive
CAA records via the dev.judgemind.org CNAME chain, preventing ACM
from issuing certificates (CAA_ERROR). The new dev.api pattern avoids
the Vercel CNAME entirely — the CA tree-walks to api.judgemind.org
then judgemind.org, where our Amazon CAA records allow issuance.

Documents the domain naming convention in the architecture spec:
- Production: {service}.judgemind.org
- Non-production: {env}.{service}.judgemind.org

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add download-db.sh for free-tier Vercel deployment (#12)

Builds free-tier DB from seed on Vercel via GitHub releases download.
Updates vercel.json buildCommand and .vercelignore to include the script.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(automation): add PR review feedback automation and CI failure monitoring

- Add automatic PR review feedback processing
  - Check formal GitHub reviews (CHANGES_REQUESTED state)
  - Check PR comments for critical feedback keywords
  - Bypass cooldown when review feedback detected
  - Iterate until all reviews approved (max 5 attempts)

- Add CI Worker for automated failure investigation
  - Monitor CI failures across repositories
  - Analyze failures (flaky vs real)
  - Auto-retry flaky tests with gh run rerun
  - Create Linear issues for real failures

- Add PR comment checking alongside formal reviews
  - getPRComments function for general PR comments
  - Detect critical keywords (🔴, critical, bug, etc.)

- Enhance dashboard with issue management buttons
  - RESTART ALL button for stuck/failed issues
  - Individual move-to-todo buttons for backlog

- Add repoMappings support for custom repository paths
  - Fix StockServer path mapping issue

- Change worktree location from /tmp to repo-internal
  - Update .gitignore to exclude /worktree/

Refs: INT-1088

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
